frameworks.name: mixed selectivity
frameworks.one_line: single neurons in higher cortex tuned to many task variables simultaneously

frameworks.name: demixed PCA
frameworks.one_line: linear decomposition isolating components tied to one task parameter each

frameworks.name: standard PCA
frameworks.one_line: variance-maximizing baseline that mixes task dimensions together

core_question: How can population activity in higher cortex be decomposed so that each latent component reflects only one task parameter while preserving variance?
key_assumptions: task parameters (stimulus, decision, time) can be treated as separable factors
task-relevant structure is linearly recoverable from population activity
preserving variance comparable to PCA is a meaningful constraint
key_findings: dPCA separates stimulus-, decision-, and time-related components cleanly in PFC, OFC, and PPC
dPCA captures variance comparable to standard PCA while demixing parameters
standard PCA mixes task dimensions that dPCA reveals as structured
interpretations: mixed-selectivity population responses contain task-aligned structure recoverable by parameter-specific demixing
demixing exposes task-relevant geometry obscured by variance-only methods
